Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
10th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IDF eliminated Khader Jamasi, the head of Hamas' funds transfer network, along with his deputy, Muhammad Harazin.

● Following the downing of a US Army Apache helicopter by an Iranian drone, the US military  launched retaliatory airstrikes targeting military assets in southern Iran.

● Iran launched at least 4 ballistic missiles and several drones targeting US bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan.

● The US launched missile strikes on water storage facilities in Bmani, Sirik, Hormozgan province, targeting 2 reservoirs.

● UKMTO reported an exchange of fire 88 nautical miles southwest of Balhaf, Yemen, between a cargo vessel and a craft carrying 6 armed persons.

● CENTCOM disabled the Palau flagged tanker M/T Settebello in the Gulf of Oman. Of the 24  Indian crew members, 21 were rescued and 3 remain missing.

● A Qatari delegation arrived in Tehran for talks on bilateral relations and regional developments, including efforts to end the war.

● As of today, CENTCOM has disabled 8 non-compliant vessels, redirected 134 ships that  complied, and allowed 42 vessels supporting humanitarian aid to pass.

END OF THE BRIEF

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

The Indian Army is set to raise dedicated Electronic Warfare (EW) Brigades focused on the borders with Pakistan and China.

These formations will be tasked with intercepting, tracking and jamming enemy communications, radars and UAVs, while integrating signal intelligence and electronic attack capabilities under a unified command structure.

The move follows lessons from recent drone-centric conflicts and is part of the Army’s broader modernisation effort. New capabilities include upgraded Dharashakti EW systems and mobile ground-based EW platforms designed to support frontline formations.

Electronic warfare is increasingly becoming a decisive component of modern conflict, enabling forces to disrupt and degrade enemy systems before kinetic operations begin.

BREAKING: Indian Army seeks to procure 300 K9 Vajra artillery guns at Rs 23,000 crore

The Indian Army is set to seek approval for the procurement of over 300 additional K9 Vajra-T 155mm/52-calibre self-propelled howitzers in a deal worth approximately ₹23,000 crore.

If approved, India's total K9 Vajra fleet will exceed 500 guns, significantly boosting long-range firepower along both the western front and the Line of Actual Control (LAC) with China.

The K9 Vajra, manufactured in India by L&T under technology partnership with Hanwha Aerospace, offers high mobility, armoured protection, and "shoot-and-scoot" capability with ranges exceeding 40 km.

The most remarkable outcome is the rise in defence exports. From a modest ₹686 crore in 2013-14, exports surged to a record-breaking ₹38,424 crore in FY 2025-26 — recording more than a 56-fold increase.

Indian systems such as BrahMos missiles, Akash air defence systems, Pinaka rockets, and advanced radars are now being exported to more than 100 countries. This marks a strategic shift from being primarily an importer to an emerging exporter, strengthening both strategic autonomy and economic gains.

tweets.title:
🇮🇳 India’s defence space is aiming a new capability leap through DefSpace Challenge 6.7.

The objective: develop a monolithic Silicon Carbide (SiC) telescope that reduces satellite payload weight by ~66% (from 600 kg to 200 kg), enabling more agile and cost-effective EO satellites.

✅ <0.5 m Panchromatic imaging for high-resolution reconnaissance
✅ 4-band Multispectral imaging at 1 m resolution
✅ 8-band SWIR imaging for camouflage detection and material identification
✅ MWIR thermal imaging for night surveillance and heat-signature tracking

Vetted by ISRO-SAC, the project aims to create a sovereign capability that does not currently exist within India’s defence space ecosystem.
